summary: Iteration 24 — Telegram voice-in -> voice-out (OpenAI TTS) feature testing. 12/12 tests passed including live OpenAI TTS generation (real OGG opus bytes, ~15KB), ContextVar propagation, tg_send also-speak behavior, _handle_tg_voice set/reset flag, TTS failure resilience, and 3 regression checks (telegram /start CODE, /api/translate, /api/help/ask, /api/insights).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2796 lines — should be split into modules (telegram/, ai/, routes/) per file-size guideline (>700 LOC).
FastAPI @app.on_event('startup') is deprecated — migrate to lifespan handlers.
tg_send's also-speak triggers a full OpenAI TTS call for every reply while _TG_SPEAK is True; consider batching or dedupe for chatty flows to reduce cost/latency (1-4s per call).
test_credentials: Bearer test_session_karya1 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Webhook secret: karya-tg-hook-7f3a9c2e. QA chat_id 999000111 preserved after tests.
seed_data_creation: None. The /start CODE regression re-links QA user's telegram_chat_id to 999000111 which matches pre-test state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TTS feature verified end-to-end. OpenAI TTS via emergentintegrations.OpenAITextToSpeech(model=tts-1, voice=nova, response_format=opus) returns valid OGG bytes (~15KB for a short sentence). _TG_SPEAK ContextVar cleanly propagates through async chain and is reset in finally block. All prior iteration (20-23) regressions green.
